styles.name: Raised 100
styles.description: For slightly raised elevation to give a subtle emphasis to elements on the page background. Examples: alerts and avatars

styles.name: Overflow
styles.description: For elements that remain fixed, staying in view on scroll, to appear above scrolling content. Examples: back to top and summary action bar

groups.name: Overlays
groups.description: Overlay elevation is used for elements that are temporal and more visually distinct via higher elevation from the surface.

styles.name: Overlay 100
styles.description: For a small overlay elevation on temporal elements that are easily dismissed. Examples: popovers, dropdown menus, and picker inputs

styles.name: Overlay 200
styles.description: For an overlay elevation that gives more emphasis to larger elements that are used in conjunction with the main page. Example: flyouts

styles.name: Overlay 300
styles.description: For floating overlays that require clear visual separation. Use for interruptive elements. Examples: modals and toasts
